transcript.text: This meeting is being recorded.
Thank you.
Hey, Vivek, how's it going?
Hey, Jackson.
How's everything?
Good.
Enjoying being in one place, I presume.
Very much so.
Enjoying being in one place.
Slowly getting used to the new schedule of more to take on in Connor's absence.
Nice.
Well, has your role expanded since he's left?
Yeah, I would say so.
By a lot, okay.
Yeah, by quite a lot.
It was already expanding as he was here.
I'm taking on more of the growth and demand side of things anyway.
And so that, plus him being gone and taking over a bit more of the miscellaneous, you know, campaigns focus, all that sort of stuff, and more interfacing with different team members, definitely busy.
Well, nice.
Congratulations on that case.
I mean, I hope it's not too busy, but, you know, it's always a good thing.
Nah, it's manageable.
It's manageable.
It's good stuff.
Stuff I'm pretty familiar with.
It is just a lot to keep up with sometimes.
Yeah, no doubt.
Well, let me share my screen.
We can get started.
So, Mara...
Is out of office.
She'll be back tomorrow.
yeah.
So, it's just the two of us.
Quick updates, we staged two articles last week, and there are eight awaiting review, and we're on track to deliver seven this week.
I think the two articles we staged last week were both pillar pages.
yeah.
And to your question, I responded in Slack previously about the redirects to the URLs.
I checked.
We didn't find any ones leading to the old logs or needing redirects.
we seem good over there.
Diving into the performance side of things.
things are going quite well.
The past two weeks.
So, this is a monthly chart, of course, but we filter this weekly.
The past two weeks have actually been, like, all-time highs.
In terms of sessions.
generally, we're doing pretty well.
We're on pace to go ahead.
October overall.
Of course, there's Thanksgiving coming up.
So, you know, we'll see how that goes.
So far, so good.
These were the top performers week on week.
So it's good to see a few couple of the pillar pages are in there, which is good, you know, pretty encouraging.
The other things are, of course, mostly around metrics.
And, you know, this is a pretty interesting one, which was specification first AI development.
So we will be refreshing this this week.

It's had a pretty interesting sort of trajectory because we developed this based on, you know, we were tracking the YouTube channel, the AI engineer YouTube channel, and they started talking about TDD and that kind of stuff.
So this is one of the blogs that was an offshoot of that particular topic.
The TDD blogs themselves are not doing great, but for some reason, this blog is getting decent amount of traffic.
And SEMrush isn't actually showing much data, last I checked for this blog.
But clearly, we're getting traffic.
So we don't know which keywords are driving it because even the Google keyword data is a bit up and down at this point.
But we're kind of ahead of Google here, so pretty interesting stuff.
I just wanted to flag that in case you're hearing anything about the specification for stuff or whatever.
Nothing of note, at least not internally from what I remember.
So I think I've definitely seen a couple of YouTube videos about it, but nothing recently.
So it's interesting that it's picking up now.
Yeah.
And then, yeah, so moving on to the clusters themselves.
So last week, we hit a new high in sessions, as I said.
This week, the overall session was slightly below that.
So every single cluster technically lost traffic.
The good news is the observability.
The sort of cluster is improving in rankings and, you know, the last column there, 13.95, which is the average position of this cluster, it was 17 a few weeks back.
So we are seeing increases.
So that's good news over there.
We're, of course, going to continue, like, creating content and just focusing on refreshes there.
Regarding the top 10 updates, Arise looks like they lost a bunch of keywords, fallen to 844 from 873, whereas, yeah, not quite sure what happened there, but, you know, well, they decreased.
We're pretty much on the same level.
We're kind of hovering between 315 to 323, that kind of range, so seems to be pretty much there.
In terms of LLM visibility, the referral traffic increased week on week.
Generally, we seem to be on pace to beat last month's referrals.
Scrunch data isn't showing a huge change as such, the only change is this one on the bottom left over here, the position, it was 45, now it's 46, which is a 1% increase, so we'll take it.
But yeah, generally, the rankings across the prompt categories remain pretty much the same.

So, yeah, it's, we're kind of looking into why there hasn't been much movement in Scrunch, because it seems like, you know, we are addressing, we have content that addresses questions within the queries that we're tracking, but for some reason, we're not able to pull ahead of Brain Trust and Arise.
It's just because maybe they have a bigger content footprint, and maybe more, you know, varied assets.
So, yeah, that's kind of where thinking is going right now, in terms of why is Scrunch positioning?
It isn't moving as much.
But again, all of this is still experimental.
So we're kind of feeling our way through the dark over here.
So yeah, that's the performance review.
And yeah, that's pretty much it.
Any questions, any clarifications I can answer?
Not at the moment.
I feel like a lot of this tracks.
I think what I'm thinking about is mostly next steps on content strategy.
I feel like we can pause on a lot of the competitive pages since we've already knocked out a lot of the big ones for now.
And we're waiting for the template from design.
So once those are done, we can start.
I'll come up with a process so we can get those transferred into the new landing page format.
And that should be pretty straightforward.
So I think now, next, it will definitely...
We want to hit the evaluation engineering bucket that we've talked about.
I think it's not, I just noticed in the Airtable, it's agent evaluation engineering.
I think it's just eval engineering.
Yeah, Connor had mentioned evaluations as well.
So we figured like if we mentioned evaluations, evals is going to get picked up.
Yeah.
Yep.
Let me see.
Yeah, yeah.

And it looks like we have what is agent evaluation engineering.
I think it would be so sorry, I'll also share my screen.
So I'm not just talking and not actually showing you what I'm referencing.

it's Okay.
I think that is an important distinction.

We can, I'm a bit concerned about having only FAQ blogs because, so the background that, you know, we're seeing is that a lot of companies are producing these sort of micro blogs, know, basic collections of FAQs.
It's basically to appeal to the LLMs, essentially.
The problem is that Google is more likely to flag that as thin content or low quality content.
So, what I would recommend...
And we can always stick the FAQs at the end of the article.
So that way we still have the FAQs, but we still have a high quality sort of cohesive article up front.
would be the only thing.
That makes sense.
That makes sense.

can gain ground that way.
Is there a huge difference between – so with evaluation engineering, are we talking about more gen AI versus or – There – it's a good question.

I think evaluation engineering is going to be the broader category and is going to cover evaluations across the different subsects of different AI systems, agents included.

So I feel like there's going to be evaluation engineering for RAG systems, LLMs, et cetera, et cetera.
But agent engineering is like the subsect of it, which will be the more important one because it's kind of obviously hotter and where the space is going.

Yeah, we can have the two blogs up now.

As well, which we have a bunch of content.

This is far too hard.

I can pitch that.

Yeah, could be a good converter as well for the free product sign-up.
Just use it as a landing page essentially to incentivize sign-ups.
So that was the thinking.
Yeah, 100%.
I mean, we've increased our weekly sign-up rate exponentially, which is great, but it's also just the drop-off of usage right now is the issue.
Yeah, yeah, that tends to happen.

Just setting the stage for next week, obviously, I know we're heading into Thanksgiving right after that, but I wanted to check, like, priorities for Q1, you know, where the product is headed, because obviously we will want to sort of maximize our shots on goal in terms of the content that we're doing.
So, yeah, for next week, I was hoping, you know, if you have any material at all, if you...
Share that, or if any thoughts on that, you know, that'd be great, and then we can maybe start planning that.
Great.
Yeah.
I think that makes sense.
I am very down to do that.

think at a high level, our next big launch is going to be around Agent Protect, which is an extension of our Protect feature, but it's just going to be more agent-specific, rebranded, but very similar as far as what it does, which is mostly guardrails, like Agent.
specific guardrails.

Galileo Academy, if you can launch it.

campaigns and paid advertising is concerned, doing really well across like different sponsorship and like newsletter sponsorship channels and also pretty solid on LinkedIn as a top of funnel lead generation asset.

just seems hard to generate any sort of traffic or interest from there as opposed to, like, or evals and, you know, even reliability and testing.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Review content performance and align on next steps for strategy.](https://fathom.video/share/Hb48ZjVjU3obJcYK4vgcR82f-dxfoBHy?tab=summary&timestamp=235.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**Performance:** Sessions hit an all-time high, but Scrunch rankings are stalled. The "observability" cluster's avg. position improved from 17 to 13.95, but traffic remains low.](https://fathom.video/share/Hb48ZjVjU3obJcYK4vgcR82f-dxfoBHy?tab=summary&timestamp=278.0)
  - [**Strategy Shift:** The focus is moving from competitor pages to "evaluation engineering" content, using "evals" as a key term to capture rising search volume.](https://fathom.video/share/Hb48ZjVjU3obJcYK4vgcR82f-dxfoBHy?tab=summary&timestamp=562.0)
  - [**Q1 Priorities:** Content will support the "Agent Protect" launch and "Galileo Academy" course, with a new "Galileo Studios" video initiative starting mid-Dec/Jan.](https://fathom.video/share/Hb48ZjVjU3obJcYK4vgcR82f-dxfoBHy?tab=summary&timestamp=1292.0)
  - [**New Ideas:** A low-lift "evals" tool will be pitched to drive sign-ups, and a blog aggregating industry case studies (not Galileo's) will be explored.](https://fathom.video/share/Hb48ZjVjU3obJcYK4vgcR82f-dxfoBHy?tab=summary&timestamp=1101.0)

## Topics

### Performance Review

  - [**Overall:** Sessions hit an all-time high in the last two weeks, pacing ahead of October.](https://fathom.video/share/Hb48ZjVjU3obJcYK4vgcR82f-dxfoBHy?tab=summary&timestamp=278.0)
  - [**Top Performers:** Pillar pages and a blog on "specification first AI development" are driving traffic.](https://fathom.video/share/Hb48ZjVjU3obJcYK4vgcR82f-dxfoBHy?tab=summary&timestamp=306.0)
      - [**Insight:** This blog is outperforming TDD-related content and SEMrush data, suggesting Galileo is ahead of the curve on an emerging topic.](https://fathom.video/share/Hb48ZjVjU3obJcYK4vgcR82f-dxfoBHy?tab=summary&timestamp=319.0)
  - [**Cluster Performance:**](https://fathom.video/share/Hb48ZjVjU3obJcYK4vgcR82f-dxfoBHy?tab=summary&timestamp=401.0)
      - [**Observability:** Avg. position improved from 17 → 13.95, but traffic remains low.](https://fathom.video/share/Hb48ZjVjU3obJcYK4vgcR82f-dxfoBHy?tab=summary&timestamp=416.0)
      - [**Other Clusters:** All lost some traffic week-over-week.](https://fathom.video/share/Hb48ZjVjU3obJcYK4vgcR82f-dxfoBHy?tab=summary&timestamp=401.0)
  - [**Competitive Tracking:**](https://fathom.video/share/Hb48ZjVjU3obJcYK4vgcR82f-dxfoBHy?tab=summary&timestamp=442.0)
      - [**Arise:** Lost keywords (873 → 844).](https://fathom.video/share/Hb48ZjVjU3obJcYK4vgcR82f-dxfoBHy?tab=summary&timestamp=442.0)
      - [**Galileo:** Stable at \~315–323 keywords.](https://fathom.video/share/Hb48ZjVjU3obJcYK4vgcR82f-dxfoBHy?tab=summary&timestamp=442.0)
      - [**Scrunch:** Position stalled at 46.](https://fathom.video/share/Hb48ZjVjU3obJcYK4vgcR82f-dxfoBHy?tab=summary&timestamp=468.0)
          - [**Hypothesis:** Competitors (Brain Trust, Arise) have a larger, more varied content footprint.](https://fathom.video/share/Hb48ZjVjU3obJcYK4vgcR82f-dxfoBHy?tab=summary&timestamp=503.0)
          - [**Action:** Continue experimentation to find a breakthrough.](https://fathom.video/share/Hb48ZjVjU3obJcYK4vgcR82f-dxfoBHy?tab=summary&timestamp=503.0)

### Content Strategy & Q1 Priorities

  - [**Evaluation Engineering Content:**](https://fathom.video/share/Hb48ZjVjU3obJcYK4vgcR82f-dxfoBHy?tab=summary&timestamp=562.0)
      - [**Focus:** Shift from competitor pages to "evaluation engineering" content.](https://fathom.video/share/Hb48ZjVjU3obJcYK4vgcR82f-dxfoBHy?tab=summary&timestamp=562.0)
      - [**Keywords:** Target "eval engineering," "agent eval engineering," and "evaluation engineering."](https://fathom.video/share/Hb48ZjVjU3obJcYK4vgcR82f-dxfoBHy?tab=summary&timestamp=600.0)
      - [**Format:** Write comprehensive articles with FAQs at the end to avoid Google's "thin content" penalty.](https://fathom.video/share/Hb48ZjVjU3obJcYK4vgcR82f-dxfoBHy?tab=summary&timestamp=690.0)
      - [**Initial Articles:** "How to become an evaluation engineer" and "What is evaluation engineering."](https://fathom.video/share/Hb48ZjVjU3obJcYK4vgcR82f-dxfoBHy?tab=summary&timestamp=642.0)
  - [**"Evals" Keyword Refresh:**](https://fathom.video/share/Hb48ZjVjU3obJcYK4vgcR82f-dxfoBHy?tab=summary&timestamp=855.0)
      - [**Rationale:** "AI evals" has higher search volume than "AI evaluations" and is the colloquial term.](https://fathom.video/share/Hb48ZjVjU3obJcYK4vgcR82f-dxfoBHy?tab=summary&timestamp=855.0)
      - [**Action:** Conduct a master refresh of all "evaluation" blogs, replacing the term with "evals" to capture this traffic.](https://fathom.video/share/Hb48ZjVjU3obJcYK4vgcR82f-dxfoBHy?tab=summary&timestamp=855.0)
  - [**Q1 Strategic Initiatives:**](https://fathom.video/share/Hb48ZjVjU3obJcYK4vgcR82f-dxfoBHy?tab=summary&timestamp=1292.0)
      - [**Product Launch:** "Agent Protect" (agent-specific guardrails).](https://fathom.video/share/Hb48ZjVjU3obJcYK4vgcR82f-dxfoBHy?tab=summary&timestamp=1332.0)
      - [**Education:** "Galileo Academy" course on evaluations to define the "evaluation engineering" category.](https://fathom.video/share/Hb48ZjVjU3obJcYK4vgcR82f-dxfoBHy?tab=summary&timestamp=1352.0)
      - [**Video:** "Galileo Studios" (2–3 technical videos/week) to launch mid-Dec/Jan, pending hiring.](https://fathom.video/share/Hb48ZjVjU3obJcYK4vgcR82f-dxfoBHy?tab=summary&timestamp=1415.0)
      - [**Sponsorship:** Galileo will sponsor Connor's "Chain of Thought" brand in 2025.](https://fathom.video/share/Hb48ZjVjU3obJcYK4vgcR82f-dxfoBHy?tab=summary&timestamp=1424.0)
  - [**PPC & Content Synergy:**](https://fathom.video/share/Hb48ZjVjU3obJcYK4vgcR82f-dxfoBHy?tab=summary&timestamp=1481.0)
      - [**E-book:** "Context Engineering" is performing well on LinkedIn and sponsorships.](https://fathom.video/share/Hb48ZjVjU3obJcYK4vgcR82f-dxfoBHy?tab=summary&timestamp=1481.0)
      - [**Search Ads:** A new agency will run broad-match ads for "AI observability platforms."](https://fathom.video/share/Hb48ZjVjU3obJcYK4vgcR82f-dxfoBHy?tab=summary&timestamp=1549.0)
      - [**Opportunity:** Explore more "context engineering" content angles.](https://fathom.video/share/Hb48ZjVjU3obJcYK4vgcR82f-dxfoBHy?tab=summary&timestamp=1517.0)

### New Content Ideas

  - [**Free Tools:**](https://fathom.video/share/Hb48ZjVjU3obJcYK4vgcR82f-dxfoBHy?tab=summary&timestamp=1101.0)
      - [**Challenge:** Most proposed tools are not feasible due to product bandwidth constraints and current free-tier onboarding issues.](https://fathom.video/share/Hb48ZjVjU3obJcYK4vgcR82f-dxfoBHy?tab=summary&timestamp=1101.0)
      - [**Opportunity:** A low-lift "evals" tool is feasible and could serve as a high-converting landing page to drive sign-ups. Jackson will pitch this internally.](https://fathom.video/share/Hb48ZjVjU3obJcYK4vgcR82f-dxfoBHy?tab=summary&timestamp=1101.0)
  - [**Industry Case Study Blog:**](https://fathom.video/share/Hb48ZjVjU3obJcYK4vgcR82f-dxfoBHy?tab=summary&timestamp=1705.0)
      - [**Concept:** A single blog post aggregating public case studies from other companies (e.g., GitLab), not Galileo's own.](https://fathom.video/share/Hb48ZjVjU3obJcYK4vgcR82f-dxfoBHy?tab=summary&timestamp=1705.0)
      - [**Rationale:** This format has proven successful for a competitor in driving traffic and citations. Vivek will research its viability.](https://fathom.video/share/Hb48ZjVjU3obJcYK4vgcR82f-dxfoBHy?tab=summary&timestamp=1705.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**Vivek:**](https://fathom.video/share/Hb48ZjVjU3obJcYK4vgcR82f-dxfoBHy?tab=summary&timestamp=1809.0)

      - [Research the industry case study blog concept.](https://fathom.video/share/Hb48ZjVjU3obJcYK4vgcR82f-dxfoBHy?tab=summary&timestamp=1705.0)
  - [**Jackson:**](https://fathom.video/share/Hb48ZjVjU3obJcYK4vgcR82f-dxfoBHy?tab=summary&timestamp=1820.0)
      - [Pitch the low-lift "evals" tool internally.](https://fathom.video/share/Hb48ZjVjU3obJcYK4vgcR82f-dxfoBHy?tab=summary&timestamp=1101.0)
      - [Add Q1 strategic priorities to the content Airtable.](https://fathom.video/share/Hb48ZjVjU3obJcYK4vgcR82f-dxfoBHy?tab=summary&timestamp=1292.0)
